item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am I missing something?

The picture says "SCP-4427 was in contact with 65 drivers before Foundation personnel were able to close the right lane"

But doesn't this de-manifest after it contacts 45 humans "…until exactly 45 people have passed through the volume of space it occupies, at which point it will disappear."

Also, is the number 45 significant?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To me this is a thing that appears randomly and does random things (mostly horrible ones) to random people. Perhaps I do not know the site well enough, if there is meant to be a context I cannot find it. From a craftsmanship point of view I can find nothing wrong with it, but the idea doesn't appeal to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Dialogue is one of my favorite things to write, so I'm always glad when it's effective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bless you for writing an interview that provides exposition while still sounding like plausible human language.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see where you're coming from. It's definitely a piece connected in a web of other narratives, and maybe too dependent on their implications. I seem to be bad about that lately, especially with this being the last piece in my Broken Masquerade puzzle. I'll think on ways to make it feel more standalone; I might just have to bite the bullet and make it longer with another addendum. Thank you for the feedback!

Edit: Edits made. There's now an Addendum I and the interview has been changed to Addendum II. I think this should help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm upvoting because this is written very well, and the dialogue is great, but I don't feel like much happens. I clicked that Nobody link, and I imagine that if I read that tale I would get some good context for this piece, but as is it just doesn't hit many notable… notes.

We have a sphere that gives people powers, and clearly attempts to touch as many people as possible, and we have it set in a broken masquerade scenario, and the link (and tags) tell me that Nobody is involved. The most mystery we really get let onto is the fact that he feels like there's a hole in his head when he thinks about the sphere…

But that plot point feels like the beginning of a big mystery, and this is certainly written to feel like it sets that up, but instead it ends then and there. I'm left wanting more, but not in a good way. And thus… well, still upvote, because damn you're good at dialogue, but you know. I felt the need to leave a comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hot hole in the sky,
Let's see what it makes you do.
Just hope you don't die.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So, 4427-B is a character from a few tales in this canon (originally 4427, changed to 5527 because I thought I wouldn't get the slot in time, I guess now it's gonna change back… oops) and I'd been stumped on writing them because I already have a collab in the works about a public human skip figure and we don't need two of those. But this was a concept I wanted to do that I felt could be frightening in this setting, and I realized I could accomplish some two-in-one action on my canonical to-do list.

Thank you to Kitsun3, stormbreath, and Gabriel Jade for draft feedback. Thank you to 9Volt and DarkStuff for post-posting feedback.

Image source is all me — took a picture outside and did the rest with the paintbrush in Photoshop. CC-by-SA-3.0 and all that.

See this post for canonical info. I should note that I think multiple nobodies exist and they don't all exhibit the same behavior; I know this may seem a little chaotic neutral of them to do. Keep in mind that this may not be an intended result, and we don't really know what 4427 is supposed to be. Delve into Doomsday Contest stuff like Headache and Paperpushing or further canon stuff like Ad Astra Per Aspera for more of what all this nobody and 3848 business is about.
